topic.what: SRAM PUF-based device fingerprint authentication for constrained IIoT using Hamming code error correction and temporal majority voting
topic.how: Combines TMV and Hamming code EC with threshold-based BER acceptance to stabilize unreliable SRAM PUF responses for authentication; defines SMec as design budget for resource-aware threshold calibration
topic.why_matters: Enables lightweight hardware-rooted authentication for resource-constrained IIoT without traditional asymmetric cryptography; provides systematic trade-off analysis between reliability, security, and computational overhead

characteristics.what_it_measures: Bit error rate after authentication stabilization
characteristics.vs_baseline: Raw SRAM PUF without EC shows higher BER
characteristics.context: Mean BER capped below 1% with proper MV and HC configuration

characteristics.what_it_measures: Bit bias in PUF response (Hamming weight)
characteristics.context: Approximates ideal 50% uniformity; MV and HC tighten distribution

characteristics.what_it_measures: Operating window between FAR and FRR thresholds
characteristics.context: Defined as tau_max minus tau_min; serves as design budget
Analytical impostor model; empirical multi-board testing remains future work
Alpha_FRR set to 0.01 conservatively due to limited sample size

characteristics.what_it_measures: Time for EC computation and MV
characteristics.value: MV dominates; up to 600ms for N=10 with boot
characteristics.context: MV boot overhead is primary energy driver; HC EC has minor time impact

characteristics.what_it_measures: Helper data bits for Hamming code
characteristics.vs_baseline: Lower code rate means more parity bits
characteristics.context: Trade-off between reliability and NVS storage; H(7,4) most redundant

characteristics.vs_baseline: Higher code rate means worse reliability
characteristics.context: H(7,4) lowest rate but best correction; H(22,16) highest rate but least correction
